title: Bacton V-9097 Memorial
crew_summary: 4 killed including Plt Off. John MacGillivray
memorial_details: | Field | Detail | |-------|--------| | Type | Crash site memorial | | Location | Bacton, Norfolk |

external_narratives.summary: TracesOfWar records a memorial at Bacton, Norfolk commemorating four RAF airmen who died when their Lockheed Hudson crashed south of the village on 16 January 1942. The aircraft was carrying four bombs at the time of the crash.
key_details: Memorial at crash site south of Bacton

external_narratives.summary: Aviation enthusiast forum discussion documenting the RAF Hudson crash near Bacton on 16 January 1942, with contributor research into the crew and circumstances.

external_narratives.source_site: Geograph - East Anglia WW2 Aircrew Crash Memorials
external_narratives.summary: Geograph's comprehensive listing of East Anglia WW2 aircrew crash memorials includes the Bacton Hudson memorial among the Norfolk sites documented.
key_details: Listed in comprehensive East Anglia WW2 memorial catalogue

local_contacts.org_name: North Walsham & District Royal British Legion

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep

sources: TracesOfWar.com — Memorial Crash Lockheed Hudson, Bacton
location_confidence.verification_notes: Current coords (52.8441, 1.4599) are 530m SW of actual location. Memorial is a marble-front brick cairn at Lowlands Farm, Hall Farm Road, Bacton NR12 0JN — private farmland south of village. Rededicated 4 November 2005. IWM ref 108252 + TracesOfWar agree on correct location.
